review_comment:
maybe switch this to be: `"%" PRIu8 ".%" PRIu8 …`

i.e. use the right printf types for this?

`struct in_addr` is `uint32_t`, and `IPV4_ADDRESS_FMT_VAL()` simply picks up its part. So, at least, the type is not `uint8_t` but `uint32_t`...

Moreover, in general, we do not usually use `"%hhu"` or `"%"PRIu8` for `unsigned char` or `uint8_t`. See e.g. `in_addr_prefix_to_string()`. And when I tried to change `%u` -> `%hhu` at somewhere previously, then you disagreed that change, hehe :-)
